subject: Project Management
title: The Deepwater Horizon
description: Learn The Deepwater Horizon in one minute.

hook.text: On April 20, 2010, an oil rig in the Gulf of Mexico exploded. Eleven workers died instantly.

buildup.text: BP had cut corners to save time and money. Safety tests were skipped or misinterpreted.

discovery.text: A blowout preventer — the last line of defense — failed because it had never been properly maintained.

twist.text: Oil gushed for 87 days. 4.9 million barrels polluted the Gulf — the worst marine oil spill in history.

climax.text: BP paid $65 billion in cleanup and fines — the price of ignored safety procedures.

punchline.text: Cutting corners cost more than doing it right ever would.

quiz.question: What was the primary cause of the Deepwater Horizon disaster?
options: Safety procedures were skipped and equipment was poorly maintained
The well was drilled in the wrong location
